explanations.8: The group ID 1000 is actually not root—root is ID 0—but this is the standard first user/group ID in Linux containers, giving them permission to own files.
explanations.11: Similarly, user ID 1000 is the first non-root user ID, which is safer than running containers as root for security reasons.
explanations.14: The username is set to 'python' here, but this is just a label for who owns the processes running in the container—it doesn't have to match the application language.
